title: hieracium piliferum subsp. piliferum
intro: Hieracium piliferum subsp. piliferum, also known as Pilifer Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, with a characteristic hairy stem and yellow flower heads. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Pilifer Hawkweed are said to include wound healing, reducing inflammation, and alleviating digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
